items: The previous session exposed heavy selling in chipmakers and Alphabet despite positive breadth.
Before the open, chip weakness, rising oil on Middle East tension, and a sharp post-earnings Netflix decline formed a three-part risk-off setup.
VIX had jumped and the 10-year yield was near 4.54%, while Iran-war risk remained unresolved.

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: AI and chip valuation concerns intensified, with Nvidia a major drag.
Brent crude surged on Iran-war concerns.
Netflix's earnings reaction added pressure to growth sentiment, and the S&P 500 recorded a 1% decline.
causal_narrative: The session converted a narrow technology warning into a broader risk-off move. Persistent semiconductor liquidation, a negative megacap earnings shock and higher oil aligned against the index. Unlike July 16, the selling was strong enough to overwhelm breadth and produce the week's decisive downside close.
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
